india_trends.title: JSW Steel posts 13-fold rise in profit for March quarter; to double capacity in 6 years
india_trends.summary: JSW Steel reported a nearly 13-fold surge in Q4 profit driven by an exceptional gain and record volumes. The company plans aggressive expansion, targeting 78 million tonne capacity by FY32, supported by JVs, higher capex and strong domestic steel demand growth outlook.

india_trends.title: SAIL Q4 Results: Cons PAT surges 47% YoY to Rs 1,835 crore, revenue rises 5%
india_trends.summary: SAIL reported a strong Q4FY26 performance with net profit rising 47% year on year and revenue growing 5%. Sequential profit surged sharply, EBITDA improved significantly, and the board recommended a dividend of Rs 2.35 per share despite mixed global steel production trends and output softness.

india_trends.title: India's exports to China rise 27 pc in Apr; imports up 20.85 pc
india_trends.summary: India's trade with China saw significant growth in April. Exports to China surged by 27 percent, while imports increased by over 20 percent. This has led to a wider trade deficit with Beijing. China has now become India's largest trading partner. Meanwhile, trade with the US also saw changes. Other

india_trends.title: SAIL Q4 Results: Net profit jumps 47% YoY to ₹1,836 crore; declares final dividend of ₹2.35 per share
india_trends.summary: Steel Authority of India (SAIL) reported a 47% year-on-year jump in consolidated net profit for the March quarter at ₹1,836 crore. In the corresponding period last year, the PSU steelmaker had posted a net profit of ₹1,251 crore.

india_trends.title: Can PM Modi’s five-nation tour help India tackle the crude oil shock?
india_trends.summary: India's energy security concerns rise as crude oil prices remain high. PM Modi's five-nation tour aims to secure long-term energy supply, starting with agreements in the UAE. Experts caution that while this may alleviate short-term panic, geopolitical risks will keep prices volatile.

india_trends.title: India, UK working on British steel issue to operationalise trade pact: Commerce Secy
india_trends.summary: India and the United Kingdom are nearing the operationalization of their trade pact. Both nations are collaborating on a unique solution to address recent UK steel safeguard measures. These measures had created a hurdle for the Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement. The pact aims to boost bilat

india_trends.title: Pulse of the Street: Indian equities fall as global headwinds and domestic woes bite
india_trends.summary: Benchmark indices posted their worst weekly slide in two months as the energy shock and record-low rupee rattled investor sentiments.

india_trends.title: SEBI expands use of borrowings for highly leveraged InvITs
india_trends.summary: The move allows funds to be used for capital expenditure, capacity expansion, major maintenance, and refinancing of principal debt under specified conditions

india_trends.title: Adanis agree to pay $18 million to settle SEC case linked to Adani Green bond offering
india_trends.summary: Without admitting or denying the allegations, Gautam Adani and Sagar Adani consented to permanent injunctions barring them from violating Section 17(a) of the Securities Act of 1933, Section 10(b) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, and Rule 10b-5.

india_trends.title: India's exports hit record USD 863 bn despite global turmoil; on track for USD 2 trillion target by 2030: Piyush Goyal
india_trends.summary: India's exports touched a record USD 863 billion in FY26 despite global geopolitical tensions and disruptions in international trade, Commerce and Industry Minister Piyush Goyal said on Friday, asserting that the country remains on track to achieve its USD 2 trillion exports target by 2030.

india_trends.title: Government gives final nod to Emirates NBD's $3 billion majority stake in RBL Bank
india_trends.summary: Dubai's Emirates NBD has secured all necessary approvals for its significant investment in RBL Bank. india_trends.title: India's BRICS trade surges to $416 billion, but widening deficit raises policy imperative ahead of 2026 Summit
india_trends.summary: India's trade with BRICS nations shows strong growth. However, a widening trade deficit is a major concern. Imports from BRICS are rising faster than exports. Russia is a key import source. India aims to balance import dependencies and boost outbound shipments. The expanded BRICS bloc holds signific

india_trends.title: Sebi, CBDT ease PAN rules for foreign investors after onboarding concerns
india_trends.summary: Sebi and CBDT relaxed PAN compliance requirements for foreign portfolio investors after concerns over complex onboarding rules.
